The '''Model G8''' was a military [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] produced by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]]

==Description==
Manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]], the Model G8 was military-grade [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]].<ref name="AEG" /> The weapon had a maximum range of three hundred and fifty [[Meter/Legends|meters]];<ref name="RASB2" /> however, the greater range of the weapon meant that the Model G8 was more expensive than equivalent blaster rifles, retailing for between 1,100<ref name="AEG" /> and 1,250 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="RASB" /> Weighing four and a half [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]], the Model G8's housing was also more fragile and did not stand up to extensive wear and tear.<ref name="AEG" />

==History==
Produced during the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Model G8 was a weapon popular with [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police forces]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|305|G8}}</ref> and authorities around the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]] and [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="AEG" /> During the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the Republic, the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Drokka]] exhibited the weapon at the [[Cularin Trade Show]].<ref name="FP">{{LivingForce|For Fun and Profit}}</ref>

During the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], these institutions around the Tion and Hutt Space sold the weapons on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], causing the Model G8 to be a common weapon on the battlefields, though not to the extent of the [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]]. Alliance [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] found that the longer range of the Model G8 allowed them to maintain the advantage in ambushes against [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]],<ref name="AEG" /> although they were not as reliable or versatile as the E-11.<ref name="CSWE" />
